User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the BookYourBrands CRM, encompassing admins, team members, and clients.

role.description: The user's role within the system (admin, team, client).

profilePic.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project managed within the BookYourBrands CRM.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client. (Relationship: Client 1:N Project)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was created.

status.description: The current status of the project (e.g., 'In Progress', 'Completed').

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task within a project in the BookYourBrands CRM.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project. (Relationship: Project 1:N Task)

assignedTo.description: Reference to the User (Team Member). (Relationship: User 1:N Task)

dueDate.description: The date the task is due.

status.description: The current status of the task (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

Chat.title: Chat
Chat.description: Represents a chat room associated with a specific project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat room (likely matches the project ID).

projectId.description: Reference to the Project. (Relationship: Project 1:1 Chat)

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message within a chat room.

chatId.description: Reference to the Chat. (Relationship: Chat 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

text.description: The text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

fileURL.description: URL of any file attached to the message.

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a file uploaded and managed within the BookYourBrands CRM.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project. (Relationship: Project 1:N File)

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

fileURL.description: URL of the file stored in Firebase Storage.

uploadTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the file was uploaded.

definition.description: Stores user information. The `userId` parameter identifies the specific user.

Stores project information. The `projectId` parameter identifies the specific project. Each project document also serves as a root for related subcollections (tasks, chats, files). Includes denormalized 'members' map for authorization independence.
Stores tasks associated with a specific project. Includes denormalized 'members' map from the parent 'project' document for authorization independence.
Stores chat information related to a project. The `chatId` should ideally match the `projectId`. Includes denormalized 'members' map from the parent 'project' document for authorization independence.

params.name: chatId
params.description: Unique identifier for the chat (should match the projectId).

definition.description: Stores messages within a specific chat.  This enables real-time chat functionality.

params.name: chatId
params.description: Unique identifier for the chat (should match the projectId).

definition.description: Stores files associated with a specific project. Includes denormalized 'members' map from the parent 'project' document for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to store data for the BookYourBrands CRM, focusing on projects, tasks, chats, and file management.  The structure emphasizes Authorization Independence by denormalizing data, particularly for collaborative data access.  This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which improves security and simplifies data access.  Structural Segregation is employed by keeping data with similar access control requirements in the same collections. Access Modeling follows a consistent pattern; User-owned data is stored using hierarchical paths (e.g., `/users/{userId}/...`), and collaborative data utilizes Membership Maps. The structure also supports QAPs by ensuring that `list` operations can be secured effectively.

To support authorization independence, the `members` map of the Project is copied into the subcollections `tasks`, `chats` and `files`.
